title: pyrus pyraster f. dasyphylla
intro: Pyrus pyraster f. dasyphylla, also known as wild pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pyrus pyraster f. dasyphylla include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health benefits.
